sentences.sentence: In a land swept by typhoons and shaken by earthquakes, how have Japan's tallest and seemingly flimsiest old buildings - 500 or so wooden pagodas - remained standing for centuries?
sentences.translation: 在一个饱受台风和地震侵袭的国度，日本最高、看似最脆弱的古建筑——约500座木塔——为何能屹立数百年？

structure.text: In a land swept by typhoons and shaken by earthquakes
Japan's tallest and seemingly flimsiest old buildings
500 or so wooden pagodas
how have Japan's tallest and seemingly flimsiest old buildings - 500 or so wooden pagodas - remained standing for centuries

difficult_points: swept 和 shaken 是过去分词，描述土地遭受的自然灾害。
how have... 是疑问倒装，remain standing 表示持续屹立。

sentences.sentence: Records show that only two have collapsed during the past 1400 years.
sentences.translation: 记录显示，过去1400年只有两座倒塌。

structure.text: Records
show
that only two have collapsed during the past 1400 years

difficult_points: that 从句说明记录内容；two 代替 two pagodas。
现在完成时与 during the past... 表示过去至今。

sentences.sentence: Those that have disappeared were destroyed by fire as a result of lightning or civil war.
sentences.translation: 那些消失的木塔是因闪电或内战引发的火灾被毁的。

structure.text: Those that have disappeared
that have disappeared
were destroyed by fire as a result of lightning or civil war

difficult_points: Those 指代 pagodas，that 从句修饰 those。
were destroyed 是被动；as a result of 引出火灾原因。

sentences.sentence: The disastrous Hanshin earthquake in 1995 killed 6,400 people, toppled elevated highways, flattened office blocks and devastated the port area of Kobe.
sentences.translation: 1995年的阪神大地震造成6400人死亡，掀翻高架公路，夷平办公楼，并摧毁神户港区。

structure.text: The disastrous Hanshin earthquake in 1995
killed 6,400 people, toppled elevated highways, flattened office blocks and devastated the port area of Kobe

difficult_points: 四个过去式动词并列，展现地震的连续破坏。
elevated highways 中 elevated 表示高架的。

sentences.sentence: Yet it left the magnificent five-storey pagoda at the Toji temple in nearby Kyoto unscathed, though it levelled a number of buildings in the neighbourhood.
sentences.translation: 然而，它让附近京都东寺那座壮丽的五层塔毫发无损，尽管夷平了周边许多建筑。

structure.text: it
left the magnificent five-storey pagoda at the Toji temple in nearby Kyoto unscathed
though it levelled a number of buildings in the neighbourhood

difficult_points: leave + 宾语 + 形容词补语表示使其保持某状态。
though 引导让步从句，it 指 earthquake。

sentences.sentence: Japanese scholars have been mystified for ages about why these tall, slender buildings are so stable.
sentences.translation: 长期以来，日本学者一直困惑于这些高而纤细的建筑为何如此稳定。

structure.text: Japanese scholars
have been mystified for ages
about why these tall, slender buildings are so stable

difficult_points: 现在完成时被动表示困惑延续至今。
why 从句作 about 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: It was only thirty years ago that the building industry felt confident enough to erect office blocks of steel and reinforced concrete that had more than a dozen floors.
sentences.translation: 直到30年前，建筑业才有信心建造拥有十多层的钢筋混凝土办公楼。

structure.text: It was only thirty years ago that
the building industry felt confident enough to erect office blocks of steel and reinforced concrete
that had more than a dozen floors

difficult_points: It was...that... 是强调句，强调时间。
confident enough to erect 表示有足够信心建造。

sentences.sentence: With its special shock absorbers to dampen the effect of sudden sideways movements from an earthquake, the thirty-six-storey Kasumigaseki building in central Tokyo - Japan's first skyscraper - was considered a masterpiece of modern engineering when it was built in 1968.
sentences.translation: 东京市中心36层的霞关大楼——日本第一座摩天楼——配有减弱地震突然侧向运动影响的特殊减震器，1968年建成时被视为现代工程杰作。

structure.text: With its special shock absorbers to dampen the effect of sudden sideways movements from an earthquake
the thirty-six-storey Kasumigaseki building in central Tokyo
Japan's first skyscraper
was considered a masterpiece of modern engineering
when it was built in 1968

difficult_points: With 短语说明建筑的减震配置；to dampen 表示目的。
was considered 是被动，when 从句说明建成时间。

sentences.sentence: Yet in 826, with only pegs and wedges to keep his wooden structure upright, the master builder Kobo Daishi had no hesitation in sending his majestic Toji pagoda soaring fifty-five metres into the sky - nearly half as high as the Kasumigaseki skyscraper built some eleven centuries later.
sentences.translation: 然而826年，建筑大师Kobo Daishi仅凭木楔和榫钉保持结构直立，就毫不犹豫地让雄伟的东寺塔冲入55米高空——几乎是11个世纪后建成的霞关大楼一半高。

structure.text: in 826
with only pegs and wedges to keep his wooden structure upright
the master builder Kobo Daishi
had no hesitation in sending his majestic Toji pagoda soaring fifty-five metres into the sky
nearly half as high as the Kasumigaseki skyscraper built some eleven centuries later

difficult_points: with + 名词 + to do 说明保持结构直立的工具。
have no hesitation in doing 表示毫不犹豫；built 修饰 skyscraper。

sentences.sentence: Clearly, Japanese carpenters of the day knew a few tricks about allowing a building to sway and settle itself rather than fight nature's forces.
sentences.translation: 显然，当时的日本木匠懂得一些让建筑摇摆并自行稳定、而不是对抗自然力量的诀窍。

structure.text: Japanese carpenters of the day
knew
a few tricks about allowing a building to sway and settle itself rather than fight nature's forces
allowing a building to sway and settle itself

difficult_points: about 后接动名词 allowing；rather than 对比两种策略。
settle itself 表示建筑自行恢复稳定。

sentences.sentence: But what sort of tricks?
sentences.translation: 但究竟是什么诀窍呢？

structure.text: what sort of tricks

difficult_points: 省略疑问句主干，完整意思是 what sort of tricks were they。
sort of 表示类型。

sentences.sentence: The multi-storey pagoda came to Japan from China in the sixth century.
sentences.translation: 多层佛塔在6世纪从中国传入日本。

structure.text: The multi-storey pagoda
came to Japan from China
in the sixth century

difficult_points: come to Japan from China 同时说明目的地和来源。
in the sixth century 限定时间。

sentences.sentence: As in China, they were first introduced with Buddhism and were attached to important temples.
sentences.translation: 和中国一样，它们最初伴随佛教传入，并附属于重要寺庙。

structure.text: As in China
they
were first introduced with Buddhism and were attached to important temples

difficult_points: they 指 pagodas；两个 were 被动谓语并列。
As in China 表示传播背景相似。

sentences.sentence: The Chinese built their pagodas in brick or stone, with inner staircases, and used them in later centuries mainly as watchtowers.
sentences.translation: 中国人用砖或石头建塔，设有内部楼梯，后来主要把它们用作瞭望塔。

structure.text: The Chinese
built their pagodas in brick or stone, with inner staircases
and used them in later centuries mainly as watchtowers

difficult_points: with inner staircases 补充建筑配置；and 连接 built 与 used。
use A as B 表示把塔用作瞭望塔。

sentences.sentence: When the pagoda reached Japan, however, its architecture was freely adapted to local conditions - they were built less high, typically five rather than nine storeys, made mainly of wood and the staircase was dispensed with because the Japanese pagoda did not have any practical use but became more of an art object.
sentences.translation: 然而佛塔传到日本后，其建筑被自由调整以适应当地条件：高度降低，通常五层而非九层，主要用木材建造，并省去楼梯，因为日本佛塔没有实用功能，而更像艺术品。

structure.text: When the pagoda reached Japan
its architecture was freely adapted to local conditions
they were built less high, typically five rather than nine storeys, made mainly of wood and the staircase was dispensed with
because the Japanese pagoda did not have any practical use but became more of an art object

difficult_points: 破折号后列举多项改造；because 从句解释省去楼梯原因。
rather than 比较楼层；be dispensed with 表示被省略。

sentences.sentence: Because of the typhoons that batter Japan in the summer, Japanese builders learned to extend the eaves of buildings further beyond the walls.
sentences.translation: 由于夏季袭击日本的台风，日本建筑师学会把建筑屋檐延伸得更远。

structure.text: Because of the typhoons that batter Japan in the summer
that batter Japan in the summer
Japanese builders
learned to extend the eaves of buildings further beyond the walls

difficult_points: that 修饰 typhoons；learn to extend 表示逐渐掌握做法。
beyond the walls 说明屋檐伸出墙体。

sentences.sentence: This prevents rainwater gushing down the walls.
sentences.translation: 这能防止雨水沿墙冲下。

structure.text: This
prevents
rainwater gushing down the walls

difficult_points: prevent + 宾语 + 动名词表示防止某事发生。
This 指延伸屋檐的设计。

sentences.sentence: Pagodas in China and Korea have nothing like the overhang that is found on pagodas in Japan.
sentences.translation: 中国和韩国的佛塔完全没有日本佛塔那样的外伸屋檐。

structure.text: Pagodas in China and Korea
have nothing like
the overhang that is found on pagodas in Japan
that is found on pagodas in Japan

difficult_points: have nothing like 表示完全没有类似的东西。
that 修饰 overhang。

sentences.sentence: The roof of a Japanese temple building can be made to overhang the sides of the structure by fifty per cent or more of the building's overall width.
sentences.translation: 日本寺庙建筑的屋顶可以伸出结构两侧，达到建筑总宽度的50%甚至更多。

structure.text: The roof of a Japanese temple building
can be made to overhang
the sides of the structure
by fifty per cent or more of the building's overall width

difficult_points: can be made to overhang 是使役被动结构。
by 表示伸出幅度。

sentences.sentence: For the same reason, the builders of Japanese pagodas seem to have further increased their weight by choosing to cover these extended eaves not with the porcelain tiles of many Chinese pagodas but with much heavier earthenware tiles.
sentences.translation: 出于同样原因，日本佛塔建造者似乎通过选择用更重的陶瓦而非许多中国佛塔的瓷瓦覆盖延伸屋檐，进一步增加了重量。

structure.text: For the same reason
the builders of Japanese pagodas
seem to have further increased their weight
by choosing to cover these extended eaves not with the porcelain tiles of many Chinese pagodas but with much heavier earthenware tiles

difficult_points: seem to have increased 表示对过去行为的推测。
not with A but with B 对比两种瓦片。

sentences.sentence: But this does not totally explain the great resilience of Japanese pagodas.
sentences.translation: 但这还不能完全解释日本佛塔的强韧性。

structure.text: this
does not totally explain
the great resilience of Japanese pagodas

difficult_points: this 指前文屋檐和瓦片增加重量的设计。
resilience 表示抵抗冲击并恢复的能力。

sentences.sentence: Is the answer that, like a tall pine tree, the Japanese pagoda - with its massive trunk-like central pillar known as shinbashira - simply flexes and sways during a typhoon or earthquake?
sentences.translation: 答案是否在于：日本佛塔像高大松树一样，凭借称为shinbashira的树干般中央巨柱，在台风或地震中弯曲摇摆？

structure.text: Is the answer that, like a tall pine tree, the Japanese pagoda - with its massive trunk-like central pillar known as shinbashira - simply flexes and sways during a typhoon or earthquake
like a tall pine tree
with its massive trunk-like central pillar known as shinbashira
known as shinbashira

difficult_points: 疑问句中 that 从句说明可能答案；with 短语补充结构。
flexes and sways 两个动词并列，描述弹性反应。

sentences.sentence: For centuries, many thought so.
sentences.translation: 数百年来，许多人都这样认为。

structure.text: For centuries
many
thought so

difficult_points: thought so 中 so 代替前句假设。
for centuries 表示长期观点。

sentences.sentence: But the answer is not so simple because the startling thing is that the shinbashira actually carries no load at all.
sentences.translation: 但答案并不简单，因为令人震惊的是，shinbashira实际上完全不承重。

structure.text: the answer
is not so simple
because the startling thing is that the shinbashira actually carries no load at all
that the shinbashira actually carries no load at all

difficult_points: because 从句说明不简单的原因；that 从句作表语。
no load at all 强调完全不承重。

sentences.sentence: In fact, in some pagoda designs, it does not even rest on the ground, but is suspended from the top of the pagoda - hanging loosely down through the middle of the building.
sentences.translation: 事实上，在一些佛塔设计中，它甚至不落地，而是从塔顶悬挂下来，松松地垂过建筑中央。

structure.text: in some pagoda designs
it does not even rest on the ground, but is suspended from the top of the pagoda
hanging loosely down through the middle of the building

difficult_points: it 指 shinbashira；not...but... 对比落地与悬挂。
hanging 分词补充悬挂状态。

sentences.sentence: The weight of the building is supported entirely by twelve outer and four inner columns.
sentences.translation: 建筑重量完全由十二根外柱和四根内柱支撑。

structure.text: The weight of the building
is supported entirely by
twelve outer and four inner columns

difficult_points: is supported by 是被动结构；entirely 强调全部。
outer 与 inner 对比柱子位置。

sentences.sentence: And what is the role of the shinbashira, the central pillar?
sentences.translation: 那么，中央柱shinbashira的作用是什么？

structure.text: what is the role of the shinbashira, the central pillar
the central pillar

difficult_points: 疑问句询问 shinbashira 的功能；同位语解释其身份。
the central pillar 是 shinbashira 的同位说明。

sentences.sentence: The best way to understand the shinbashira's role is to watch a video made by Shuzo Ishida, a structural engineer at Kyoto Institute of Technology.
sentences.translation: 理解shinbashira作用的最佳方法，是观看京都工艺纤维大学结构工程师Shuzo Ishida制作的视频。

structure.text: The best way to understand the shinbashira's role
is to watch a video made by Shuzo Ishida
a structural engineer at Kyoto Institute of Technology

difficult_points: to understand 修饰 way；is to watch 是表语不定式。
made by 修饰 video，同位语说明 Ishida 身份。

sentences.sentence: Mr Ishida, known to his students as 'Professor Pagoda' because of his passion to understand the pagoda, has built a series of models and tested them on a 'shake-table' in his laboratory.
sentences.translation: Ishida先生因热衷理解佛塔而被学生称为“佛塔教授”，他在实验室制作了一系列模型并在“振动台”上测试。

structure.text: Mr Ishida
known to his students as 'Professor Pagoda' because of his passion to understand the pagoda
has built a series of models and tested them on a 'shake-table' in his laboratory

difficult_points: known as 是过去分词短语；because of 引出称号原因。
has built 与 tested 并列，表示实验活动。

sentences.sentence: In short, the shinbashira was acting like an enormous stationary pendulum.
sentences.translation: 简言之，shinbashira就像一个巨大的固定摆。

structure.text: In short
the shinbashira
was acting like an enormous stationary pendulum

difficult_points: 过去进行时描述模型实验中正在发挥的作用。
act like 表示功能类似。

sentences.sentence: The ancient craftsmen, apparently without the assistance of very advanced mathematics, seemed to grasp the principles that were, more than a thousand years later, applied in the construction of Japan's first skyscraper.
sentences.translation: 古代工匠似乎没有高等数学帮助就掌握了这些原理，而一千多年后这些原理被应用于日本第一座摩天楼的建造。

structure.text: The ancient craftsmen
apparently without the assistance of very advanced mathematics
seemed to grasp the principles
that were, more than a thousand years later, applied in the construction of Japan's first skyscraper

difficult_points: seem to grasp 表示推测；that 修饰 principles。
were applied 是被动，插入时间说明应用更晚。

sentences.sentence: What those early craftsmen had found by trial and error was that under pressure a pagoda's loose stack of floors could be made to slither to and fro independent of one another.
sentences.translation: 早期工匠通过反复试验发现，在压力下，佛塔松散叠放的楼层可以彼此独立地来回滑动。

structure.text: What those early craftsmen had found by trial and error
was
that under pressure a pagoda's loose stack of floors could be made to slither to and fro independent of one another
independent of one another

difficult_points: What 从句作主语；that 从句作表语。
could be made to slither 是使役被动，independent 表示彼此独立。

sentences.sentence: Viewed from the side, the pagoda seemed to be doing a snake dance - with each consecutive floor moving in the opposite direction to its neighbours above and below.
sentences.translation: 从侧面看，佛塔仿佛在跳蛇舞——每一层都向上下邻层相反的方向移动。

structure.text: Viewed from the side
the pagoda
seemed to be doing a snake dance
with each consecutive floor moving in the opposite direction to its neighbours above and below

difficult_points: Viewed from the side 是观察角度；seem to be doing 表示看起来正在。
with 独立结构描述各层相对运动。

sentences.sentence: The shinbashira, running up through a hole in the centre of the building, constrained individual storeys from moving too far because, after moving a certain distance, they banged into it, transmitting energy away along the column.
sentences.translation: shinbashira从建筑中央孔洞贯穿而上，限制各层移动过远，因为移动一定距离后便会撞上它，把能量沿柱子传走。

structure.text: The shinbashira
running up through a hole in the centre of the building
constrained individual storeys from moving too far
because, after moving a certain distance, they banged into it
transmitting energy away along the column

difficult_points: constrain from doing 表示限制；they 指 storeys。
after moving 省略主语；transmitting 分词表示撞击结果。

sentences.sentence: Another strange feature of the Japanese pagoda is that, because the building tapers, with each successive floor plan being smaller than the one below, none of the vertical pillars that carry the weight of the building is connected to its corresponding pillar above.
sentences.translation: 日本佛塔另一个奇特特征是，由于建筑逐层变窄，每层平面都比下层小，承重的垂直柱没有一根与上方对应柱相连。

structure.text: Another strange feature of the Japanese pagoda

is that, because the building tapers, with each successive floor plan being smaller than the one below, none of the vertical pillars that carry the weight of the building is connected to its corresponding pillar above
because the building tapers
with each successive floor plan being smaller than the one below
that carry the weight of the building

difficult_points: that 从句说明 feature；because 与 with 结构共同解释逐层变小。
none of the pillars 作单数概念，谓语 is connected。

sentences.sentence: In other words, a five-storey pagoda contains not even one pillar that travels right up through the building to carry the structural loads from the top to the bottom.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，五层佛塔甚至没有一根柱子贯穿全楼、承担从顶部到底部的结构荷载。

structure.text: a five-storey pagoda
contains not even one pillar
that travels right up through the building to carry the structural loads from the top to the bottom
to carry the structural loads from the top to the bottom

difficult_points: not even one 强调一根也没有；that 修饰 pillar。
to carry 表示贯穿的功能目的。

sentences.sentence: More surprising is the fact that the individual storeys of a Japanese pagoda, unlike their counterparts elsewhere, are not actually connected to each other.
sentences.translation: 更令人惊讶的是，日本佛塔各层不像其他地方的佛塔那样，实际上彼此并不相连。

structure.text: More surprising
is
the fact that the individual storeys of a Japanese pagoda, unlike their counterparts elsewhere, are not actually connected to each other
unlike their counterparts elsewhere

difficult_points: 表语 More surprising 前置，主语是 fact。
are not connected 是被动，unlike 表示对比。

sentences.sentence: They are simply stacked one on top of another like a pile of hats.
sentences.translation: 它们只是像一摞帽子一样一层叠在另一层上。

structure.text: They
are simply stacked one on top of another
like a pile of hats

difficult_points: are stacked 是被动，强调各层的放置方式。
one on top of another 表示逐层叠放。

sentences.sentence: Interestingly, such a design would not be permitted under current Japanese building regulations.
sentences.translation: 有趣的是，按照日本现行建筑法规，这种设计不会获准。

structure.text: Interestingly
such a design
would not be permitted under current Japanese building regulations

difficult_points: would not be permitted 是假设性被动。
under regulations 表示依据法规。

sentences.sentence: And the extra-wide eaves?
sentences.translation: 那么，超宽屋檐呢？

structure.text: the extra-wide eaves

difficult_points: 省略疑问句，询问屋檐的作用。
extra-wide 作复合定语。

sentences.sentence: Think of them as a tightrope walker's balancing pole.
sentences.translation: 把它们想成走钢丝者的平衡杆。

structure.text: Think of them as
a tightrope walker's balancing pole

difficult_points: think of A as B 表示把A看作B。
them 指 extra-wide eaves。

sentences.sentence: The bigger the mass at each end of the pole, the easier it is for the tightrope walker to maintain his or her balance.
sentences.translation: 杆两端的质量越大，走钢丝者越容易保持平衡。

structure.text: The bigger the mass at each end of the pole
the easier it is for the tightrope walker to maintain his or her balance
for the tightrope walker to maintain his or her balance

difficult_points: the more..., the more... 表示越……越……。
it is for...to... 是形式主语结构。

sentences.sentence: The same holds true for a pagoda.
sentences.translation: 佛塔也是同样。

structure.text: The same
holds true
for a pagoda

difficult_points: hold true 表示同样适用；the same 指前句平衡原理。
for a pagoda 表示这一平衡规律也适用于佛塔。

sentences.sentence: 'With the eaves extending out on all sides like balancing poles,' says Mr Ishida, 'the building responds to even the most powerful jolt of an earthquake with a graceful swaying, never an abrupt shaking.' Here again, Japanese master builders of a thousand years ago anticipated concepts of modern structural engineering.
sentences.translation: “屋檐向四面伸出，像平衡杆一样，”Ishida先生说，“建筑面对最强烈的地震冲击也会优雅摇摆，而不是突然震动。”同样，一千年前的日本建筑大师预见了现代结构工程的概念。

structure.text: With the eaves extending out on all sides like balancing poles
says Mr Ishida
the building responds to even the most powerful jolt of an earthquake with a graceful swaying, never an abrupt shaking
Japanese master builders of a thousand years ago anticipated concepts of modern structural engineering

difficult_points: with + 名词 + 分词描述屋檐状态；respond to 表示应对冲击。
never an abrupt shaking 是省略结构，与 graceful swaying 对比。
